interactions.summary: The host introduces Jiang as a forecaster and asks what method makes his geopolitical predictions work.
Jiang answers that he uses game theory and treats geopolitics as self-interested players rather than ideological actors.
Jiang lays out his 2026 frame: April US-China negotiations sit atop a long dollar architecture built through oil pricing and China’s integration into the dollar system.

claims.claim: Jiang says his forecasting method is game theory centered on self-interest rather than ideology.
Jiang predicts that the key event of 2026 will be Trump’s April state visit to China and the wider US-China bargain around it.
Jiang argues that after leaving gold, the United States stabilized the dollar through the petrodollar and by opening China into a dollar-centered trade order.
Jiang says US wars, debt expansion, and the 2008-2009 crisis pushed China to worry about dollar stability and build more sovereign financial alternatives.
Jiang argues Trump wants to force China to keep buying dollars and treasuries, including by using Venezuela and possible Iran escalation to redirect China’s energy dependence.
claims.temporal_scope: Current strategic diagnosis and 2026 forecast stated on 2026-01-05.

signature_moments.moment: The dollar order appears as a two-pillar structure of oil pricing and China’s incorporation.
signature_moments.source_phrase: The first is he established the petrol dollar ... and the second thing that he did was he visited China to open up China
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It compresses Jiang’s whole opening world-model into a memorable architecture rather than a generic US-China rivalry summary.

signature_moments.moment: Trump’s 2026 strategy is framed as coercing China back into dollar dependence through war geography.
signature_moments.source_phrase: if Trump were to attack Iran and destabilize the Middle East, then China will be forced
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the interview its first sharp causal chain linking hemispheric war moves to monetary leverage.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang uses this as the first post-gold mechanism that gave the floating US dollar value by forcing oil purchases into dollars.
Jiang uses this for China’s attempt to recalibrate finance away from unstable dollar dependence.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang grounds the January 5, 2026 forecast in a chronology running from 1971 dollar delinking, through 1980s China opening, to post-2008 Chinese financial repositioning.

uncertainty_notes.note: Some financial terms are noisy in ASR, especially around yuan internationalization and named institutions, but the broad claim is clear.
